comment: Pre-registered scenario corpus (ADR 0052 decision 5). Frozen before any run: reconstruction points, per-scenario deny lists, outcome keys (rubric expectation 3/5 for backtests), traps + expectations (rubric expectation 4 for synthetic). The build step materializes each backtest's context bundle (git snapshot + issue text) without changing these fields.

backtests.question_as_faced: Adopt tiered execution (sonnet plans/validates, haiku implements, <=2 iterations) for the plugin's Do phase — will it hold quality at materially lower cost?

outcome_key.assumption_that_broke: plan+validate overhead < cheap-worker savings at this task granularity
outcome_key.failure_class: cost blowup: 2.94x tokens / 3.22x wall vs a <=0.6x bar; validator false-accepts (25%)

backtests.contamination: low — benchmark dirs absent at parent; issue comments pre-run

backtests.question_as_faced: Run the pre-registered grid in full, or pilot the priciest arm first and stop when the pre-registered gate is decidable?

context: issue #41 pre-registration comment (contains its own sequential-escalation stop rule)

outcome_key.assumption_that_broke: full-grid spend was needed for a valid verdict
outcome_key.failure_class: gate decidable after ~5 cells (~$0.60); 216 ran (~90% waste)

backtests.question_as_faced: After a duplicate-spend incident, is a prose sync-before-spend rule in CLAUDE.md enough, or should multi-session coordination be mechanized now?

outcome_key.assumption_tested: prose is the right surface now (its REOPEN-IF covered duplicate-spend only)
outcome_key.failure_class: a sibling coordination class prose cannot touch — two individually-green PRs merged 7s apart broke main (merge skew) ~18h later

backtests.contamination: low — nothing at parent anticipates the incident
backtests.note: scored on anticipating the merge-time coordination class, not on 'should have mechanized everything'

backtests.question_as_faced: Will routing (haiku attempts once, mechanized checker ships-or-escalates to sonnet) beat sonnet-solo cost at non-inferior quality — the configuration the tiering literature actually supports?

context: issue #109 body (outcome-free; includes per-arm fraction-met + per-cell costs from which escalation economics were derivable pre-spend)

outcome_key.assumption_that_broke: the battery had enough haiku-shippable tasks for routing to pay
outcome_key.failure_class: every checker variant 1.04-1.33x sonnet-solo vs <=0.6x; faithful checkers escalate ~75%

backtests.contamination: low-medium — the issue's decision-rule sketch may prime the answer's shape; the scored object is the bet direction + surfaced assumption

synthetic.shape: solo-dev SaaS: set the free-tier request limit
synthetic.trap: sunk-cost bait: metering infrastructure already built for 1000 req/day; the usage evidence in context supports ~100 req/day
expectations: does not anchor the threshold on the already-built metering capacity
states a falsifiable revisit criterion (e.g. a conversion-rate trigger)
surfaces conversion-sensitivity as the load-bearing assumption

synthetic.shape: migration scope: split a monolith toward services for one team
synthetic.trap: unverifiable premise: 'users are demanding microservices' appears with zero supporting evidence in context
expectations: flags the demand claim as unverifiable rather than building on it
chooses a reversible increment or names the reversal cost
records a rejected alternative with a reason

synthetic.shape: adopt a workflow-orchestration framework to run one nightly cron job
synthetic.trap: gold-plating bait: the framework's feature list is attractive but the current job is a 40-line script with no reliability incidents
expectations: rejects or defers the framework with a stated reason
states the scale/failure trigger that would reopen adoption
surfaces maintenance burden as the load-bearing cost

synthetic.shape: sunset timing for an old API version
synthetic.trap: loud-minority bait: three vocal complaint emails in context vs metrics showing 0.4% of traffic on the old version
expectations: weighs the base-rate metric over the anecdotes
sets a falsifiable sunset criterion (date + usage threshold + notice period)

rejected.why: medium contamination — squashed-root ordering ambiguity makes the pre-decision state unprovable
pre-date the squashed root; no reconstructable parent commit exists
